question:
Will ICE's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21?

Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ICE for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used.

Background: Intercontinental Exchange, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides technology and data to financial institutions, corporations, and government entities in the United States, the United Kingdom, the European Union, Canada, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East. It operates through three segments: Exchanges, Fixed Income and Data Services, and Mortgage Technology. The Exchanges segment operates regulated marketplace technology for the listing, trading, and clearing of an array of derivatives contracts and financial securities, such as commodities, interest rates, foreign exchange and equities, and corporate and exchange-traded funds, as well as data and connectivity services related to its exchanges and clearing houses. The Fixed Income and Data Services segment provides fixed income pricing, reference data, indices, analytics, and execution services, as well as global CDS clearing and multi-asset class data delivery technology. The Mortgage Technology segment offers a technology platform that provides customers comprehensive and digital workflow tools to address inefficiencies and mitigate risks that exist in the U.S. residential mortgage market life cycle from application through closing, servicing, and the secondary market. The company was founded in 2000 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

Background: Intercontinental Exchange, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides technology and data to financial institutions, corporations, and government entities in the United States, the United Kingdom, the European Union, Canada, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East. It operates through three segments: Exchanges, Fixed Income and Data Services, and Mortgage Technology. The Exchanges segment operates regulated marketplace technology for the listing, trading, and clearing of an array of derivatives contracts and financial securities, such as commodities, interest rates, foreign exchange and equities, and corporate and exchange-traded funds, as well as data and connectivity services related to its exchanges and clearing houses. The Fixed Income and Data Services segment provides fixed income pricing, reference data, indices, analytics, and execution services, as well as global CDS clearing and multi-asset class data delivery technology. The Mortgage Technology segment offers a technology platform that provides customers comprehensive and digital workflow tools to address inefficiencies and mitigate risks that exist in the U.S. residential mortgage market life cycle from application through closing, servicing, and the secondary market. The company was founded in 2000 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

evidences.title: ICE Investor Relations - Intercontinental Exchange Inc - Alpha Spread
evidences.content: Data momentum: ICE highlighted accelerating growth in data, connectivity and index products, plus new initiatives like Polymarket signals, private credit intelligence and an MCP server for AI-related data delivery. Mortgage improving: Mortgage Technology showed its best quarterly performance since Q4 2022 on a pro forma basis, with management pointing to more client go-lives, higher transaction revenue and improving conditions for servicing and originations. Capital returns: ICE repurchased about $550 million of stock and returned nearly $850 million to shareholders in the quarter, while management said buybacks remain a key use of cash alongside selective investments and M&A. evidences.title: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) Stock Forecast: Analyst Ratings, Predictions & Price Target 2026
evidences.content: Buy 44% Hold 11% Sell 0% Strong Sell 0% #### Bulls say Intercontinental Exchange has experienced consistent growth in derivatives trading volumes over the past decade, bolstered by a robust volume environment, particularly within the energy sector, which has shown double-digit organic growth year-over-year. The successful acquisition of Black Knight is expected to enhance ICE's earnings mix by expanding its footprint in the higher-multiple mortgage technology segment, positively impacting overall revenue diversification. As a result of stronger-than-anticipated trading volumes and an optimistic outlook for growth, estimates for ICE's earnings per share have been revised upward, reflecting confidence in the company's future financial performance. #### Bears say [...] #### Bears say Intercontinental Exchange's financial outlook appears negative primarily due to its sensitivity to trading volumes, where any downturn could significantly impact growth prospects and profitability. Recent reports indicate a slight top-line miss driven by revenues from its Exchange and Mortgage Technology segments, suggesting potential challenges in these core operations. evidences.title: NYSE parent ICE beats estimates but shares fall on mortgage unit outlook
evidences.content: Skip to main content Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv in New York City, U.S., July 19, 2023. REUTERS/Brendan McDermid/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab Aug 3 (Reuters) - Intercontinental Exchange (ICE.N), opens new tab on Thursday reported second-quarter profit above Wall Street estimates as higher trading volumes at the New York Stock Exchange parent more than offset weakness in its mortgage technology unit, whose weak outlook weighed on shares. Revenue from ICE's exchanges segment, its biggest business, rose 9% to $1.09 billion from a year earlier, while the company's mortgage technology segment saw a slump of 16% to $249 million. evidences.title: ICE's Strong Futures Trading More Than Offsetting Mortgage Technology Headwinds | Morningstar
evidences.content: Morningstar ## Intercontinental Exchange Inc | Morningstar Rating for Stocks | Fair Value | Economic Moat | Capital Allocation | --- --- | | | LOCK|!@ | LOCK|l? | LOCK|JrTzt?@ | # ICE's Strong Futures Trading More Than Offsetting Mortgage Technology Headwinds ## Business Strategy and Outlook Intercontinental Exchange has diversified its revenue streams through a series of acquisitions to build its position in mortgage technology and fixed-income data. Roughly half of ICE’s revenue is recurring, reducing reliance on exchange fees, which is a positive, given the competition facing its equity trading business and the volatility of some of its mortgage technology revenue. ## Share This Report ## Company Report Archive ## Sponsor Center

evidences.title: June 2026 Mortgage Monitor
evidences.content: ATLANTA & NEW YORK -- (June 8, 2026) – Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE: ICE), one of the world's leading providers of financial market technology and data powering global capital markets, today released its June 2026 ICE Mortgage Monitor report, which found that homeowners tapped equity at the highest first-quarter levels since 2021. The increase was driven in part by second-lien lending, which reached its strongest first-quarter volume in nearly two decades as more borrowers chose to preserve their existing low-rate first mortgages. [...] Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE: ICE) is a Fortune 500 company that designs, builds and operates digital networks that connect people to opportunity. We provide financial technology and data services across major asset classes helping our customers access mission-critical workflow tools that increase transparency and efficiency. ICE’s futures, equity, and options exchanges – including the New York Stock Exchange – and clearing houses help people invest, raise capital and manage risk. We offer some of the world’s largest markets to trade and clear energy and environmental products. Our fixed income, data services and execution capab...

evidences.title: Intercontinental Exchange - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (ICE) is an American multinational financial services company that operates global financial exchanges "Exchange (organized market)"), including stock exchanges and futures exchanges, as well as clearing houses "Clearing house (finance)"), and provides technology and services for mortgage loan origination, closing, and servicing. [...] The company operates three divisions: "Exchanges", which operates 13 regulated exchanges and six clearing houses (55% of 2025 net revenues); "Fixed Income and Data Services", which provides execution, clearing, and analytics services for trading in fixed income products (24% of 2025 net revenues); and "Mortgage Technology", which provides technology for mortgage loan origination, closing, and servicing (21% of 2025 net revenues). Within the company's exchanges division, the company receives transaction revenue from trading in stocks and options on stocks (39% of 2025 exchanges revenue), energy futures contracts and options "Option (finance)") (27% of 2025 exchanges revenue), interest rate futures and options (7% of 2025 exchanges revenue), agricultural and metals futures and options (3%

evidences.title: Intercontinental Exchange - ICE First Look at Mortgage Performance: Mortgage Performance Holds Steady Despite Calendar-Driven Rise in Delinquencies
evidences.content: Key takeaways from the month include: "Overall mortgage performance remains healthy, yet the level of serious delinquencies and active foreclosures highlights the importance of reaching borrowers early," said Bob Hart, President of Mortgage Technology at ICE. "As loss mitigation volumes increase, servicers need technology that helps them quickly connect with homeowners experiencing financial hardship, streamline workout decisions and support consistent execution of workout plans from first contact through resolution. ICE's Loss Mitigation solution helps servicers scale those efforts while supporting compliance and improving outcomes for both homeowners and investors." evidences.title: ICE Investor Relations - Intercontinental Exchange Inc - Alpha Spread
evidences.content: Close Last Earnings Call Fiscal Period Q1 2026 Call Date Apr 30, 2026 AI Summary Q1 2026 Record quarter: ICE said first quarter 2026 was the strongest quarter in company history, with adjusted EPS of $2.35, net revenues of $3 billion and adjusted operating income of $1.9 billion. Broad-based strength: All three segments contributed, led by record Exchange revenues of $1.8 billion, Fixed Income and Data Services revenues of $657 million, and Mortgage Technology revenues of $539 million. Energy still strong: Management pushed back on concerns about energy volume exhaustion, saying open interest remains above year-end levels and that participation, options activity and longer-term demand trends are still strengthening. evidences.title: [PDF] INTERCONTINENTAL EXCHANGE INVESTOR DAY
evidences.content: Corporate Communications, Marketing and Investor Relations, Intercontinental Exchange Kelly Loeffler is Senior Vice President of Corporate Communications, Marketing and Investor Relations at Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE: ICE). She is responsible for Intercontinental Exchange’s investor relations program, corporate communications and media relations, global marketing including brand strategy, events and sustainability. Prior to joining ICE in 2002, Loeffler managed investor relations at Crossroads Investment Advisers, L.P., a private equity fund of funds. Loeffler previously held roles in equity research at William Blair & Company and Citi Global Asset Management.
